# Introduction

The manubot pattern — manuscripts as software, citations by persistent
identifier, immutable per-commit permalinks, collaboration through pull
requests — has been used for hundreds of preprints since
[@doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128]. The pattern's value is well-established;
its main historical limitation is that it shipped as a single
opinionated toolchain bound to one project shape.

This book chapter cites the foundational manubot paper above without
any setup beyond declaring the extension in `_quarto.yml`. No
`references.bib` entry, no manual lookup — pandoc-manubot-cite resolves
the DOI against Crossref on render, writes the CSL JSON entry to
`references.json`, and caches the metadata in `_freeze/manubot-cache.json`
so subsequent renders stay offline.

For a contrast, `@quarto2024` references a hand-curated entry from
`references.bib`. Both kinds of citation render through the same
pandoc citeproc pipeline and appear together in the bibliography.
